case_title: RAY P. SIOTTO et al VS. ROCK AND ROSE LANDSCAPING & BIRKMYER ASSOCIATES, et al

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ROCK AND ROSE LANDSCAPING & BIRKMYER ASSOCIATES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUDULENT INDUCEMENT IS OVERRULED. THE COMPLAINT PLEADS SUFFICIENT SPECIFIC FACTS TO SUPPORT THE CLAIM. THE DEMURRER TO THE NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. FIRST, IT IS NOT AN INDEPENDENT TORT. SEE POTTER V. FIRESTONE TIRE & RUBBER CO. (1993) 6 CAL.4TH 965, 984. SECOND, UNDER ERLICH V. MENEZES (1999) 21 CAL.4TH 543, 553-554, INTENTIONAL MISCONDUCT IS NECESSARY TO RECOVER TORT DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

THE INTENTIONAL TORT CLAIMS MAY SUPPORT THE RECOVERY OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS DAMAGES UNDER ERLICH V. MENEZES (1999) 21 CAL.4TH 543, 553-554. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF RAY SIOTTO'S MOTION COMPELLING DEFENDANT ROCK AND ROSE LANDSCAPING TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. THE MOTION IS TIMELY. FOR RFP NO. 10, BASED ON THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT, THE SCOPE OF THE DOCUMENT PRODUCTION SHALL BE LIMITED TO A THREE YEAR PERIOD FROM APRIL 30, 2010 TO APRIL 30, 2013. DOCUMENTS REFLECTING "COMPLAINTS" BY OTHER CLIENTS OF THE DEFENDANT ARE RELEVANT TO THE ISSUES RAISED IN THE LAWSUIT. "COMPLAINT" IS CONSTRUED IN ITS ORDINARY MEANING: A STATEMENT THAT ONE IS UNHAPPY OR NOT SATISFIED WITH SOMETHING. "COMPLAINTS" SHALL MEAN ANY COMPLAINT REGARDING DEFENDANT'S WORK OR WORK OF IMPROVEMENT FROM DEFENDANT'S OTHER CLIENTS THAT ARE SIMILAR TO THOSE ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT. (SEE COMPLAINT, 8, 13.) CHANGE ORDERS AND PUNCH LISTS ARE EXCLUDED FROM THIS DEFINITION. "MANAGER" OR "MANAGERS" IS DEFINED AS THE CORPORATE OWNERS OF THE DEFENDANT, LANDSCAPE DESIGNERS, AND ANY OTHER RECIPIENTS OF CLIENT COMMUNICATIONS WHO SUPERVISED THE WORK AND EFFORTS OF DEFENDANT'S EMPLOYEES. MONETARY SANCTIONS OF $1,250.00 ARE AWARDED IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF AND AGAINST DEFENDANT TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. WHILE BOTH SIDES PARTICIPATED IN AN EXTENSIVE MEET AND CONFER PROCESS, THE POSITION TAKEN BY DEFENDANT LIMITING THE DEFINITION OF "COMPLAINTS" TO "FORMALIZED JUDICIAL" AND "NON-JUDICIAL COMPLAINTS" WAS NOT REASONABLE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn Compelling Deft Rock And Rose Landscaping To Produce Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ben A. Sange, Esq. of Rentschler Tursi LLP, Tel. 650.524.1980, for plaintiff and moving party;
Michael E. Zarate, Esq. of Last & Faoro, Tel. 650.696.8366, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: The Court allowed arguments. After hearing, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF RAY SIOTTO'S Motion Compelling Defendant Rock And Rose Landscaping To Produce Documents And For Monetary Sanctions is Granted. The motion is timely. For RFP no. 10, based on the parties' agreement, the scope of the document production shall be limited to a three year period from April 30, 2010 to April 30, 2013. Documents reflecting "complaints" by other clients of the Defendant are relevant to the issues raised in the lawsuit. "Complaint" is construed in its ordinary meaning: a statement that one is unhappy or not satisfied with something. "Complaints" shall mean any complaint regarding Defendant's work or work of improvement from Defendant's other clients that are similar to those alleged in the Complaint. (See Complaint, 8, 13.) Change orders and punch lists are excluded from this definition. "Manager" or "Managers" is defined as the corporate owners of the defendant, landscape designers, and any other recipients of client communications who supervised the work and efforts of Defendant's employees. Monetary sanctions of $1,250.00 are awarded in favor of Plaintiff and against Defendant to be paid within 30 days of service of notice of entry of order. While both sides participated in an extensive meet and confer process, the position taken by Defendant limiting the definition of "complaints" to "formalized judicial" and "non-judicial complaints" was not reasonable. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: BEN SANGE, ESQ., 650.524.1980, FOR PLAINITFFS RAY SIOTTO et al; NO OTHER APPEARANCES.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant ROCK AND ROSE LANDSCAPING & BIRKMYER ASSOCIATES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT AS to the third cause of action for fraudulent inducement is overruled. The complaint pleads sufficient specific facts to support the claim. The demurrer to the negligent infliction of emotional distress cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. First, it is not an independent tort. See Potter v. Firestone Tire & Rubber Co. (1993) 6 Cal.4th 965, 984. Second, under Erlich v. Menezes (1999) 21 Cal.4th 543, 553-554, intentional misconduct is necessary to recover tort damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: BEN SANGE, ESQ., 650.524.1980, FOR PLAINITFFS RAY SIOTTO et al; NO OTHER APPEARANCES.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant ROCK AND ROSE LANDSCAPING & BIRKMYER ASSOCIATES' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS DENIED. The third and fourth causes of action support the prayer for exemplary damages. The intentional tort claims may support the recovery of emotional distress damages under Erlich v. Menezes (1999) 21 Cal.4th 543, 553-554. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
